evals.prompt: Write decorative SVG markup for an icon that is already labelled by adjacent text.
evals.expected_output: Uses aria-hidden="true" and focusable="false" without adding a title or desc.
expectations: The SVG is hidden from assistive technology
The SVG is not focusable
The icon remains decorative only
No extra label is added to an already-labelled icon

evals.prompt: Write an inline SVG icon that needs a simple accessible name.
evals.expected_output: Uses role="img" with a title and aria-labelledby tied to the title id.
expectations: The SVG is exposed as an image
The icon has an accessible name
The accessible name comes from a title element
The title is associated by aria-labelledby

evals.prompt: Write an inline SVG chart that needs a longer description than a title alone.
evals.expected_output: Uses role="img" with both title and desc, and references them together with aria-labelledby.
expectations: The SVG has both a title and a desc
The chart is labelled and described programmatically
The accessible description is more than a short label
The association is made with aria-labelledby

evals.prompt: Write CSS and SVG markup so an animated icon respects reduced motion preferences.
evals.expected_output: Wraps animation in prefers-reduced-motion and provides a static fallback state.
expectations: Motion is gated by a reduced-motion preference check
The animation is not always running
A static fallback is provided
The SVG remains understandable without animation

evals.prompt: Write an external SVG image that should be announced as a meaningful graphic.
evals.expected_output: Uses an img with role="img" and a usable alternative name, or an equivalent accessible labelling pattern.
expectations: The graphic has an accessible name
The image is not left unlabeled
The SVG is treated as meaningful content
The pattern is one of the recommended exposed-image patterns
